message:
test(e2e): add bd-db300.5.5.3 writer-routing protocol evidence harness

298-line end-to-end test under
`crates/fsqlite-e2e/tests/bd_db300_5_5_3_writer_routing_protocol.rs`
that produces protocol evidence for beads issue
`bd-db300.5.5.3` — the Track E5.2 / E5.3 writer-routing
protocol milestone. The harness does not exercise the real
MVCC stack (that's still future work); instead, it drives
the synthetic-workload evaluation framework added to
`fsqlite_mvcc::writer_routing_telemetry` in the preceding
commits and records the full baseline-vs-routed comparison
as structured JSON evidence.

## Constants and setup

- `BEAD_ID = "bd-db300.5.5.3"` — so protocol evidence can be
  cross-referenced to the beads ledger.
- `REPLAY_COMMAND = "cargo test -p fsqlite-e2e --test
  bd_db300_5_5_3_writer_routing_protocol -- --nocapture
  --test-threads=1"` — exact command the consumer of the
  evidence should run to reproduce.
- `WRITER_ROUTING_PROTOCOL_LOCK: Mutex<()>` — process-wide
  serialization so the structured evidence emission is
  deterministic even when multiple tests in the file run in
  parallel.

## Imports

Pulls in the full synthetic evaluation surface re-exported
from `fsqlite_mvcc` at crate root:
`WriterRoutingDecisionConfig`,
`WriterRoutingPlacementProfile`,
`WriterRoutingSyntheticComparison`,
`WriterRoutingSyntheticConfig`,
`WriterRoutingSyntheticSummary`,
`WriterRoutingSyntheticWorkload`, and
`compare_writer_routing_synthetic_workload`. Also imports
`serde::Serialize` and `serde_json::Value` for the structured
evidence emission.

## Structured evidence payload

Defines `RoutingProtocolFairnessSummary` (and related
evidence structs) as `#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize)]` so
the test output can be captured by the existing e2e evidence
collector. Each test in the file writes a JSON evidence block
tagged with `bead_id`, `phase`, and the scenario identifier
so the CI evidence pipeline can correlate it against the
beads tracker.

## What the tests exercise

The harness sets up the three canonical synthetic workloads
(disjoint, overlapping, hot-page contention) across the three
placement profiles (baseline unpinned, home-lane pinned,
home-node pinned) and produces a
`WriterRoutingSyntheticComparison` for each cell. It then
asserts:

1. Baseline and routed runs agree on the total number of
   writers (sanity check — both modes are required to
   process the same input).
2. Each scenario's routed latency / retry / fairness metrics
   fall within the documented protocol envelope for that
   scenario.
3. The aggregate evidence payload serializes cleanly to
   JSON and includes every field the downstream evidence
   pipeline consumes.

This is pure test code — no production behavior change — and
lives under `fsqlite-e2e` rather than inside the mvcc crate
itself so the evidence run can import the full public crate
API the same way a downstream consumer would.
